scope: extend gmd-app source-build PoC from one to two services; no contract flip, no routing, no chart change

phase_a_script_extension.rejection_behavior: exit 2 with explicit message listing the supported services
phase_a_script_extension.service_dispatch_strategy: explicit per-service entries in SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT associative arrays; no wildcard

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: feat(gmd-app): extend first-party source-build PoC to emailservice

for_chart_integration_minimal.approach: Recommended Option B from UP8-source-boundary audit: add helm.parameters to the dev element of infrastructure/gitops/gmd/applicationset.yaml flipping `images.repository` -> `host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd` and `images.tag` -> the gmd-app source-build tag for `gmd-dev` only. Canonical chart values.yaml stays untouched until all default services are proven.
for_chart_integration_minimal.blocked_until: all default first-party services build successfully on the chosen substrate (host or cluster builder)

for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.change_required: scripts/amof/api/routers/release.py release_lifecycle_action() must branch on name == 'gmd' so action == 'build' calls build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command instead of build_lifecycle_build_command
for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.blocked_until: the build command can produce the full set of gmd-deployable images, otherwise the dispatched build would always be incomplete

for_contract_flip.change_required: _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') -> source_build with produces / source_repos / build_command metadata; update lifecycle contract test that pins kind == 'mirror_upstream'
for_contract_flip.blocked_until: lifecycle dispatch wiring is in place AND the chart actually consumes the locally-built images, otherwise the unhidden Build button would build images nothing pulls

cluster_builder_substrate_decision.open_question: Does the gmd lifecycle build need to run inside the in-cluster builder pod (same path as AMOF platform substrate)?
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_yes_required_work: Mirror per-language base images so the builder pod's containerd can pull them: golang:1.26.1-alpine, gcr.io/distroless/static, python:3.14.3-alpine (for emailservice; not the same as the existing python:3.10-slim mirror), mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0.100-noble, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime-deps:10.0.0-noble-chiseled, plus the Java/Node/etc. bases of the other services.
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_no: Host-only builds suffice for local development; production / cloud-dev path remains a separate decision.

what_to_implement: Pick one more service that exercises a different language stack from frontend (Go) and emailservice (Python). Candidates ordered by rising complexity: currencyservice (Node), shippingservice (Go), recommendationservice (Python), productcatalogservice (Go), checkoutservice (Go), paymentservice (Node), adservice (Java), cartservice (.NET, nested context).
Add the chosen service to SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT in scripts/build-gmd-app.sh.
Extend _GMD_APP_BUILD_SUPPORTED_SERVICES in command_builder.py and add focused tests.
Run the host build for the new service and verify registry proof.
Still no contract flip, no routing, no chart change.
